front: Opera: set in 17th-century Venice; libretto by Arrigo Boito; title character stabs herself at the end after helping her rival Laura escape the inquisitor Alvise Badoero and his spy Barnaba
Opera: Arrigo Boito wrote its libretto (he also wrote libretti for Verdi's Otello and Falstaff and composed Mefistofele)
Opera: title character's mother La Cieca is accused of using witchcraft to influence a regatta; her daughter sings the aria 'Suicidio' before stabbing herself
Opera aria: sung by Enzo on his ship in Act II as he awaits the arrival of Laura; Carlo Bini was booed at the Met in 1982 for singing it after replacing Plácido Domingo
Ballet: from La Gioconda; set at a party at the Ca' d'Oro while Laura's husband believes she is dying; depicts dawn, day, evening, and night
Ballet: animated in Disney's Fantasia with dancing hippos and alligators; provides the melody for Allan Sherman's 'Hello Muddah, Hello Fadduh'
Composer: I Lituani is based on Adam Mickiewicz's Konrad Wallenrod (St. Petersburg premiere under the title Aldona); I Promessi Sposi is based on Manzoni's novel
